question: Which quarter shows the best overall performance based on the stacked bar chart?

options.A: Q3 shows the best performance because it has balanced metrics across all categories.
options.B: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines unrelated metrics that cannot be meaningfully added together.
options.C: Q4 shows the best performance because it has the highest stacked total.
options.D: Q1 shows the best performance because it has the lowest values for negative indicators.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a single total. These metrics have different units and meanings (e.g., counts, temperatures, consumption), so adding them together creates a meaningless sum. The stacked height cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric comparisons are valid.
